changes.before: The village was worse at street level.

changes.after: Useful and careful were separate facts.

changes.before: Whatever had emptied Millhaven had taken the people but not all their animals, which meant the leaving had dragged long enough for some creatures to scatter and ended fast enough that others had stayed to starve.
changes.after: Whatever had emptied Millhaven had taken the people and left some of their animals behind. Some creatures had scattered before it finished; others had stayed to starve.

changes.before: That was a different kind of strength than Sylvanas had first given her credit for.
On the farms two days back, Sylvanas would have called that scholar's caution, the kind that kept mages arguing while the thing in front of them kept moving.
changes.after: On the farms two days back, Sylvanas would have called that scholar's caution.

changes.before: Behind the village, Sylvanas got the rest from churned mud and broken prints.
changes.after: She found the rest behind the village, where the lane narrowed into a muddy track between garden plots.

changes.before: Jaina met Sylvanas's eyes for an instant.

changes.after: Cyndia's fingers closed around the nearest cloth marker.

changes.before: Toward people who still thought this was someone else's road.
No signet. Just the flat oval of a field mark, enough to tell the next post this had come off the road instead of out of an office.
changes.after: The flat oval of a field mark was enough to tell the next post this had come off the road instead of out of an office.

changes.before: Two rangers and a mage heading south into country that was getting worse.
changes.after: Two rangers and a mage heading south in failing light, with Corin's Crossing ahead and Millhaven at their backs.

changes.before: He had the kind of face road years had worn smooth rather than memorable.
The grain seller was still only a clue, but now two people had given Sylvanas the same one.
